title: cinna lateriflora
intro: Cinna lateriflora, also known as sweet grass or buffalo grass, is a perennial grass native to North America, belonging to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Cinna lateriflora include relieving digestive issues, calming anxiety and stress, and promoting respiratory health, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Cinna lateriflora can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused oil, or dried and smoked as a smudging plant, with the latter being used to purify and cleanse the air in sacred spaces.
